text: The hall at Tara was quiet after, and it is I that made it quiet. Murchadh mac Fáelán never raised his voice — it was the board that said what he wished said, and the board said it plain.

I am after walking out of Tara with nothing but the clothes on my back and the game still turning in my head. There are roads west — there are always roads west — and people who have not yet heard my name said wrong.

Connacht. The Atlantic comes in hard off the headlands and everything here has the smell of salt and old rain. These people have their own games, their own grievances. It is nobody I am here, and that is the right place to start from.

Fiachra let me go without a word, and it is a long while I felt his eyes at my back. The road east is longer than the one I came by. Murchadh is at the end of it, and he does not know yet that I am walking.

speaker: Murchadh mac Fáelán
text: It is your name I have heard. I had expected something more of it.

speaker: Murchadh mac Fáelán
text: Go west. There might be people out along those roads who would not yet know to refuse you.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Sit, sit — it was the net I was meaning to mend but sure it will wait. You have the look of a person who has been walking.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Ha. Well now. The fish will not mock me for it, I suppose. It is someone who has lost before that you play like — and did not forget the losing.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: There it is. I do not always see the whole board but sometimes the board will show me a thing anyway. Stay and eat, so, if you have no place to be.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Back already. I thought you might be, right enough. Here — the board is where you left it.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: It is not in haste I play, and it is not in company I do not trust. Sit with me a while first. Then we will see what there is to see.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: It is as though you have been the King yourself that you protect him. That is not a thing you would find commonly.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You opened too early. Patience is not the absence of movement — it is knowing which movement to withhold.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You have been thinking on it since. I can see that in you. Good — it is thinking that is most of the game.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: Three men I have beaten today already and the night still young on us. Set the board — I have not the whole evening to be giving you.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: Right, right. Fair enough for you. Was it Séanán sent you? He has the look of a man that does his teaching quietly.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: You held too long. A man that will not press his advantage is a man that is asking to lose. Come back when you have the stomach in you for it.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: Again. Fine so. It is not easy I will be making it this time.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: Three different things I heard about you from three different people, and not one of them agreed. Sit — it is which one is the truth I want to see.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: There is someone east of here who has not seen that yet. I wonder will you be there before the word of it travels ahead of you.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: You read the board well enough. But it is the player you are not reading, and that is where the gap is.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: You came back. That is the first thing I was needing to know about you.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: You came back quieter than you went, and you played quieter too. That is the sea's way of teaching — not with words, not with any fuss at all.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You held the thing you learned and did not let the losing spoil it. That is the harder discipline, and it is you that had it in you all along.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: All right, all right — you came back and you came back with something, I will give you that much and not a word more than that.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: The second thing — you came back and you won it. The road east is yours now.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: It was not yourself I was expecting to see here again, not after the way that went. Sit then — but easy I will not be going.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: There now. That is a thing itself. What was before — I will not be holding that against what just happened. The board has a memory of its own.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Again. I do not know what it is you are trying to prove, but the board keeps telling you it is not proved yet.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Nothing have I said to anyone about this, and I will not say it. But you keep coming back, and I keep on winning, and that much is between ourselves.

speaker: Séanán na Farraige
text: Well. There it is now. It is not in the sea's nature to stay angry — that was never the way of it. Go on, so.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: It is time I gave you, and it is the game straight I gave you. What you took from that was not what was offered. Come — we will see now if you have put it right.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: You found the discipline. That is what I was waiting for — not the win, the discipline behind it. We are done with this.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: It is the same gap, only showing in a different place. You have not found the root of it yet. I have nothing more to say on it.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: It is not with heat I hold things. It is with stillness. A long time you have had now to feel that stillness, and it has not moved you yet. Sit.

speaker: Brigid na Scailpe
text: It was the time of it I misjudged — how long it would take. That error is mine, not yours. You have the thing now. Keep it.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: I told everyone in this house you would come back and lose again. Do not be making me wrong in front of them.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: The once. In my hall. I will not be pretending that did not happen. But do not think it means more than the one time it was.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: Twice now. Walk away and do not come back to me until you have something I have not already beaten.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: It is beaten you I have enough times that your name in this hall is a story people tell for a laugh. Prove it wrong or do not sit down at all.

speaker: Tadhg an Ósta
text: You won. In my hall. In front of my people. I will not be pretending it did not happen. That is the full of what I have for you.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: It is something on your face you came back with that was not there before. It is not the board I think you are angry at. Sit — let us find out what it is.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: It was not the move I was watching for — it was what came before the move. You found something in yourself. The rest of this is finished now.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: It is something you are carrying into the game that has no business being there. Until you set it down, you will keep losing it for reasons that have nothing to do with the board.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: Nothing have I told to anyone of what I think is happening with you. There is time yet to settle it on the board. It is the last time I will be offering that.

speaker: Fiachra an Fhánaí
text: I wondered for a while would you get here. Then I stopped wondering and I started waiting. The road east is clearer now — you have settled the thing that was clouding it.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: Every road through here crosses another. It is the choice made at each crossing that says what kind of player you are. We will see about yours.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: It was the third crossing I misread — I took the road that looked open and it was you had made it look that way. That is not a small thing to observe.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: It is the choice you made on the left flank — the one you did not take — that cost you. Not the move you played. The road not followed.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: You have been back along the roads you took last time, I think. Let us see which turning you have decided on differently.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: It was the same crossroads and you took the other road. That is the whole of what this game teaches, if you let it. You have let it.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: I have written down what you did. I do not say that to unsettle you — I say it because it is the truth, and the truth is useful. Sit.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: It is the choice I have been waiting to see you make. The analysis is resolved. I will not be writing anything further on it.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: The same fork again and the same road taken. It is not stubbornness I accuse you of — stubbornness would be quicker than this.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: I have read every road you have taken at this board. I know the shape of the choice before you make it. Prove the map wrong.

speaker: Orlaith na gCros
text: You took a road I did not have written. That is the only answer to a map that knows you — make the road that is not on it yet. I see that now.

speaker: Diarmait Óg
text: I have beaten two people today. I will beat three. Set the board — I have not got time for the long way round of talking about it.

speaker: Diarmait Óg
text: That. Right. I had you and the luck went against me on the last two moves — you know that yourself. I want another game, now, straight away.

speaker: Diarmait Óg
text: I will remember that. I will remember every single move of that and I will be back for it. Do not go far.

speaker: Diarmait Óg
text: I have been waiting. I know what you did and I have the answer to it. Come on then — let us not drag it out.

speaker: Diarmait Óg
text: I had the answer wrong. I — fine. Fine. You will hear about me again. That much I know.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed the way you sit down. It tells something already. Play — I will tell you what it tells when we are done.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed that your strongest moves come after your quietest pauses. That is a thing worth knowing about yourself. I will remember it too.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed you move quickly when you are uncertain. It is the certain moves that are slow. You have it backwards. That is the thing to work on.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have been looking forward to this. I have a number of observations from last time that require testing. You may have corrected some of them.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed the uncertain moves are slower now. You have reversed the thing I said. It is not a small correction to make and it is made.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have a record of every game between us. I have noticed a pattern in it that you may not have seen yourself. Sit — let us see if I am right again.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed the pattern has not changed. That is not a judgment — it is an observation. You will want to think on the cause of it.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed something has changed. I do not yet know what it is, but the data does not lie. I will be thinking about this one.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have studied you longer than I have studied most things. I find I am interested in whether the study is enough. I think we are about to find out.

speaker: Caoimhe an Léinn
text: I have noticed the study was not enough. I find that genuinely interesting. There is a chapter of my notes that will have to be rewritten.

speaker: Conchobar Críonna
text: A long time I have been sitting at this board. A long time I have watched people come east along these roads with something to prove. Sit — I have seen your kind before.

speaker: Conchobar Críonna
text: A long time since someone surprised me at that board. I am not a man given to surprise. Take a moment — know what that means before you walk away from it.

speaker: Conchobar Críonna
text: You played bravely and you lost. There is no shame in that here. But bravery and strength are not the same stone — you will want to know the difference before you go east.

speaker: Conchobar Críonna
text: You came back. A long time I have sat here and I tell you — not all of them come back. That already says something. Let us see what else it says.

speaker: Conchobar Críonna
text: The road to Tara is long and it does not forgive what is unfinished. You are less unfinished than you were. That is as much as this old road can give you. Go on.

The roads here are old — older than the people who walk them. Every crossroads has a stone worn smooth by hands that are gone. Munster on one side, Leinster on the other, and myself in the middle of it with a name that is starting to mean something.

Conchobar said nothing when I stood up from the board, only looked at me as a man looks at weather coming in from the east. The midlands are ahead and the air is thicker there — bog and rain and people who do not give their respect cheaply.

The heart of Ireland. The bogs stretch out flat to the sky and the rain comes down soft and steady as if it means to stay. People here play differently — slower, heavier, as though the board has weight to it. My name has arrived before me.

Niall of the Nine Losses let me go with a nod and nothing more. It is enough. The road turns east now and there is no turning back from it. Tara is close — I can feel the shape of it in the land, pulling like a tide. Murchadh is there. He knows I am coming.

Leinster. The fields are greener here and the roads are straighter. Everything points to Tara. The people who play me now are not road-players — they are court-players, and the court is watching. It is not far now. It was never far. It was only that I was not ready.

The hall at Tara is quiet again. It is I that made it quiet — the same words, a different meaning. The board is between us and the pieces are still. Murchadh has not spoken. There is nothing left that needs saying. The road west brought me here and the road is finished.
